_schema_notes.coverage: Authoritative schema for all DID formats — DD Form 1664 (pre-2014) and free-text (post-2014 MIL-STD-963C)
_schema_notes.did_format: Set to 'dd_form_1664' or 'free_text'. Determines which fields are expected to be populated.
_schema_notes.963c_compliant: True if the DID was prepared under MIL-STD-963C (2014 or later). False for older DIDs.
_schema_notes.reserved_fields: Fields marked RESERVED are present in one format but not the other. Always include them — set to empty string or empty array when not applicable.
_schema_notes.preparation_instructions: Official MIL-STD-963C name for what was previously called 'requirements'. Contains the numbered sections of the DID. All section numbers are normalized to strictly numeric dot-notation (1, 1.1, 1.1.1). Letter-based source numbering (2.a, 3.1.b) is converted to numeric equivalents (2.1, 3.1.2).
_schema_notes.asterisk: True if the requirement number is followed by * in the source document (conditional requirement).

title: TEST REQUIREMENTS DOCUMENT (TRD)
summary: This DID defines the format and content for test requirements documents, applicable to engineering, preliminary qualification, qualification, and acceptance testing for data deliverables specified in solicitations.
use_relationship: This Data Item Description (DID) contains the format, content, and intended use information for the data deliverable resulting from the work task described in the solicitation. This DID is applicable to engineering (developmental), preliminary qualification, qualification, and acceptance testing.

references.label: Copies of these standards are available online at

distribution_statement: DISTRIBUTION STATEMENT A: Approved for public release; distribution is unlimited.

preparation_instructions.title: Reference documents.
preparation_instructions.content: The applicable issue of the documents cited herein, including their approval dates and dates of any applicable amendments, notices, and revisions, shall be as specified in the contract.

preparation_instructions.title: Content.
preparation_instructions.content: The TRD shall include four parts for each item being tested. All test parameters and test conditions shall be independent of any specific test equipment's. The TRD shall include the following information.

children.title: Part A – A detailed description of the performance characteristics of each system, subsystem, unit, and subassembly (referred to as Units-Under-Test (UUT)) and a summary of the total testing requirements including input conditions, outputs measurements, and test points.
Functional name of the UUT and supplier's part numbers.
All inputs and their range and tolerance.
All outputs in terms of their range, accuracy, and relationship to the input conditions.
All test points identified by test connector, pin designator, function, and signal (input and output) conditions.
children.content: This will include the specific parameters that can be measured at the test point and the value(s) expected.

children.title: Part B – The performance tests required to complete exercise the UUT in various modes of operation to reveal any degradation in performance characteristics.
Part C – The diagnostic tests and support equipment required to isolate and diagnose a fault(s) detected during the UUT performance testing.
children.content: Fault isolation will be to the lowest component part possible.

children.title: Part D – The Automatic Test Markup Language (ATML) for the Part B performance tests and the Part C diagnostic tests in accordance with Institute of Electrical and Electronic Engineers Standards 1671.1 and 1671.3.
